bullets.text: Progressive's underwriting profit margin was 8.4% in 1998 versus only 0.3% for the industry—the most profitable auto insurance period in 20 years; operating EPS reached $6.11 versus $4.46 in 1997.
Personal Lines net premiums written grew 15% to $4,922.3 million; Progressive became the 5th largest U.S. auto insurer with 4.0% market share of U.S. personal lines premiums.

Progressive expanded to 83 television advertising markets, grew to five call center locations, surpassed 200 active strategic alliances, and enabled online buying in 23 states plus D.C., leading the industry in multi-channel distribution.

Progressive introduced 'Personal Progressive' allowing online policy management, payments, and claims tracking; Internet became the fastest-growing channel; 1-800 AUTO PRO was the fastest-growing direct channel.

Progressive deployed next-generation product design in mid-1998, using credit-based rating in 43 states; early results showed attraction of more drivers from all risk profiles with longer retention; new design targeted 80% of premium by April 1999.

Progressive expanded TV advertising to 43 additional markets in 1998, growing brand promotion to 83 total markets; introduced Immediate Response Vehicles and prominent agent signage to build brand visibility.

Progressive refined Immediate Response claims service with Immediate Response Vehicles (white with blue logo) deployed nationally; hired and trained over 3,800 people companywide including claim representatives.
Debt-to-total capital ratio was 23.3%; repurchased 393,000 shares at average $104/share; filed shelf registration for $300 million senior debt; investment income after tax was $221.3 million.

Peter Lewis announced CEO succession: Chuck Chokel became CEO-Investments on January 1, 1999, with Lewis remaining CEO-Insurance Operations and Chairman; the letter described this as the best legacy contribution.

Industry profits were near peak but Lewis warned rates were declining and margins would likely turn negative industry-wide; Progressive prepared for the downturn with brand-building and efficiency investments.
